name: Invoice Automation — Email → AI → Xero → Slack

filters.q: has:attachment filename:pdf newer_than:1d from:(billing OR invoice OR accounts OR ar OR @stripe OR @xero)

nodes.name: Gmail Trigger — new invoice email
nodes.notes: Polls every minute. Filter is intentionally generous — the AI step does the real classification. Adjust 'from:' to your suppliers' patterns.

nodes.name: Extract PDF text
nodes.notes: Reads the PDF attachment to text. If your supplier sends scanned PDFs, swap this for a Google Document AI / AWS Textract / Mistral OCR node.

parameters.text:
=Extract the following fields from this invoice text. Return STRICT JSON only — no preamble, no markdown.

Fields:
- supplier_name (string)
- invoice_number (string)
- invoice_date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- due_date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- subtotal_aud (number, GST exclusive)
- gst_aud (number)
- total_aud (number, GST inclusive)
- currency (3-letter code)
- line_items (array of {description, quantity, unit_price_aud, total_aud, suggested_gl_code})
- po_number (string or null)
- bank_details: {bsb, account_number, account_name}
- payment_terms (string)
- confidence (number 0-1 — how confident you are in the extraction)

GL code hints: 4000 = revenue, 5000-5999 = COGS, 6100 = software subscriptions, 6200 = professional services, 6300 = office, 6400 = travel, 7000 = wages. Pick the closest.

Invoice text:

{{ $json.text }}

options.systemMessage: You are an Australian SMB finance assistant. Be conservative: if a field is ambiguous, prefer null over a guess. Output STRICT JSON.

nodes.name: AI Extract (invoice → structured)
nodes.notes: Use Claude Sonnet 4 or GPT-4o here — both are reliable at structured extraction. Temperature 0 for determinism.

nodes.name: Parse AI JSON output
nodes.notes: Coerce the AI's string output into a real JSON object so downstream nodes can address fields.

nodes.name: Confidence ≥ 0.85?
nodes.notes: Anything below 0.85 confidence or with a zero/negative total goes to the human-review path instead of straight to Xero.

nodes.name: Xero — create draft bill
nodes.notes: Always creates as DRAFT — a human approves before payment. If you want auto-approval for trusted suppliers, add a Set node before this that maps supplier_name to a whitelist.

parameters.text:
=:incoming_envelope: *New invoice ready for approval*
*{{ $('04-parse-json').item.json.supplier_name }}* — A${{ $('04-parse-json').item.json.total_aud }} ({{ $('04-parse-json').item.json.invoice_number }})
Due: {{ $('04-parse-json').item.json.due_date }}

<https://go.xero.com/Bills/Edit/{{ $json.BillID || $json.id }}|Review in Xero →>

nodes.name: Slack — notify #finance
nodes.notes: Posts a one-click-to-Xero card so the approver doesn't have to context-switch hunting for the bill.

parameters.text:
=:warning: *Invoice needs human review* (low confidence)
From: *{{ $('04-parse-json').item.json.supplier_name || 'Unknown supplier' }}*
Confidence: {{ ($('04-parse-json').item.json.confidence * 100).toFixed(0) }}%
Raw text snippet:
```
{{ $('02-pdf-extract').item.json.text.substring(0, 400) }}
```

nodes.name: Slack — flag for human review
nodes.notes: The human-fallback path. Cheaper to flag 10% of edge cases than to risk a bad post in Xero.

meta.description: Demo workflow showing the typical invoice-reconciliation build. Replace credential IDs and adjust the Gmail filter / GL-code hints to your business before going live.
